mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the number of employees on nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding economic activity, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any rolling 2-year period the Rank IC for PAYEMS changes on 5-day forward return is consistently below 0.03, or if the top decile of PAYEMS growth weeks show negative returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed funds rate rises above 0.25%, it signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in the 20-day window following each FEDFUNDS > 0.25% event, the S&P 500 yields positive returns more than 50% of the time over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between low UNRATE and high HOUST, indicating a strong labor market and robust housing demand, amplifies the positive signal for equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint condition is more informative than either series alone.
failure_condition: If the interaction term in a linear model of UNRATE and HOUST on 60-day forward returns has a t-statistic less than 2 in a rolling 5-year regression, or if the sign of the interaction coefficient is inconsistent with the hypothesized direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals economic strength, but its effect on equities depends on the cycle phase. In a post-recession recovery, rising production boosts earnings and S&P 500 returns. In a late-cycle phase, rising production may overheat and trigger negative returns as policy tightens.
failure_condition: If over the post-recession recovery regimes (as defined by NBER trough + 12 months), the Rank IC of INDPRO changes on 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or if the direction of returns is negative in more than 60% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending as measured by retail sales (RSAFS) directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, transmitting into higher stock prices over the following month. This relationship is persistent across economic regimes.
failure_condition: If the month-over-month change in RSAFS has a Spearman correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns of less than 0.1 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6%, it indicates a deteriorating labor market that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If in the 5 trading days after each UNRATE > 6% reading, the S&P 500 produces positive returns more than 50% of the time over the evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the Fed funds rate and inflation (CPIAUCSL) determines the real interest rate. When FEDFUNDS is low relative to CPIAUCSL (negative real rate), monetary policy is accommodative, which supports equity prices over the next month. The joint movement of these two series provides a stronger signal than either alone.
failure_condition: If the interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL (e.g., FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL) fails to show a consistent positive association with forward returns at the 20-day horizon (Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator. During early expansion phases, rising housing starts signal strengthening demand and positive equity returns. During late-cycle overheating, further increases may foreshadow a downturn and lead to negative returns.
failure_condition: If during early expansion regimes (first 12 months of NBER expansion), the Rank IC of HOUST changes on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03, or if the proportion of positive returns is not significantly above 50%.
